sourceMovie.title: Who Framed Roger Rabbit
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Who Framed Roger Rabbit (1988) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Robert Zemeckis's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scooby-Doo! Abracadabra-Doo
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Who Framed Roger Rabbit clicked for you, Scooby-Doo! Abracadabra-Doo (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Zootopia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Who Framed Roger Rabbit clicked for you, Zootopia (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Big Lebowski
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Who Framed Roger Rabbit clicked for you, The Big Lebowski (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Who Framed Roger Rabbit?
faq.answer: Try Scooby-Doo! Abracadabra-Doo, Zootopia, The Big Lebowski—each shares DNA with Who Framed Roger Rabbit while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Who Framed Roger Rabbit?
faq.answer: Who Framed Roger Rabbit (1988) is best described as Fantasy, Animation, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Who Framed Roger Rabbit worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
